meta.title: FORGE
meta.description: Communities build real-world projects the way open source builds software: in public, with claimable tasks and credited contributors, focused on a few at a time, built to outlive their founders.

home.tagline: Where ideas are forged to completion.
home.heroHeadline: Build it in public. Finish it together.
home.heroSub: Communities take on real-world projects the way open source builds software : claimable tasks, credited people, a few carried to the finish at a time.
home.spotlightSub: A few projects at a time, finished, not a thousand abandoned.
home.openSlotHint: Filled when a project becomes ready.
home.peoplesChoiceHint: The community's free vote. The only slot that can be cancelled.
home.pcVoteNow: Back a ready project for the wildcard slot
home.spotlightEmpty: No projects in the spotlight yet.
home.winsTeaser: {finished, plural, one {# finished playbook} other {# finished playbooks}} · {copies, plural, =0 {not yet copied} one {copied in # city} other {copied in # cities}}

me.title: Your projects
me.sub: FORGE has no accounts. This device remembers the projects you manage so you can jump straight back in — or paste your secret link to re-enter from anywhere.
me.resumeHint: Paste your secret manage link, or just the token from it. It's the only key — there are no accounts to recover it.

directory.title: All projects
directory.sub: Every project on FORGE — in the spotlight, ready and waiting, getting ready, or finished. Search or filter, then open one to join the crew or claim a task.
directory.results: {n, plural, =0 {No matches} one {# project} other {# projects}}
directory.noResults: No projects match. Try a different word or clear the filters.
directory.empty: No projects yet. Be the first to start one.

spotlight.title: In the spotlight
spotlight.sub: A few projects at a time, in focus and finished — three slots per duration, plus the community's People's Choice. Open slots fill the moment a project is ready.

new.title: Start a project
new.sub: Name a goal, pick a duration, line up your first tasks and crew. The checklist shows what your project needs to be ready for a spotlight.
new.fGoalHint: Concrete and finishable. From 'clean the canal bank' to 'get our school on solar'.
new.fPoolHint: How long this project should take. Longer pools ask for more structure.
new.fEmailHint: Used to dedupe crew and hand off the project. Never shown publicly.
new.fRoleHint: E.g. organizer, logistics. Roles count toward readiness on longer pools.
new.forkingFrom: Copying the playbook from “{title}”. Edit anything for your own city, then make it yours.

wins.title: Win wall
wins.sub: Finished projects become playbooks anyone can copy and run in their own city. Fork credit tracks how far each one travels.
wins.empty: No finished projects yet. The first community to close one writes the first playbook.
wins.copiedIn: Copied in {n, plural, one {# city} other {# cities}}
wins.notYetCopied: Not copied yet — be the first city.
wins.results: {n, plural, =0 {No matches} one {# finished} other {# finished}}
wins.noResults: No finished playbooks match. Try a different word.

readiness.title: To reach a spotlight
readiness.sub: Fixed bars, never votes. Meet them and the project takes the next open slot.
readiness.funding: A funding link if there are costs

crew.heading: Crew ({n})
crew.fRoleHint: What you'll help with. Counts toward readiness on longer pools.
crew.joined: You're on the crew. You can claim tasks now.
crew.requested: Request sent. The maintainer will approve you before you can claim tasks.

task.heading: Tasks
task.reportSubmitted: Submitted. The maintainer will review it.
task.reportPlaceholder: What you did. Markdown works: **bold**, - lists…

project.copiedIn: Copied in {n, plural, one {# city} other {# cities}}
project.forkFamilyHeading: This playbook is running in {n, plural, one {# city} other {# cities}}
project.forkTogether: Together: {tasks, plural, one {# task done} other {# tasks done}} · {updates, plural, one {# build-log entry} other {# build-log entries}}
project.readySub: This project meets every bar. It takes the next open slot in its pool, or its place in the lottery when slots are contested.
project.queuedSub: What this project still needs before it can take a spotlight slot.

manage.taskHint: Something a crew member can finish in a couple of hours.
manage.noTasks: No tasks yet. Add a few so people can help.
manage.logHint: What you did and why. The first entry is part of readiness.
manage.videoHint: A short video of the work. Shown on the public page. Paste a YouTube link, or clear it to remove.
manage.closeHint: What got done. This becomes the playbook on the win wall.
manage.closed: Project closed. It now lives on the win wall.
manage.closedNotice: This project is finished and lives on the win wall.
manage.tokenBody: This URL is the only key to manage your project. There are no accounts to recover it. Copy it somewhere safe.
manage.handoffBody: You stepped back. This link makes the new maintainer — send it to them. It's the only key to manage the project, and your old link no longer works.
manage.adoptedBody: You're the maintainer now. Save this link — it's the only key to manage the project. There are no accounts to recover it.
manage.deniedBody: Managing a project needs its secret link. Open the manage link you saved when you created it.

pc.title: People's Choice
pc.lead: Every other spotlight is earned by readiness and a fair lottery — reach can't buy them. This one slot is the exception: the community's free vote. Back the ready projects you believe in (one vote each); when the slot is free, the most-backed project takes it and keeps it until it finishes.
pc.noHolder: The slot is open. A ready project needs {n} votes to claim it — back one below.
pc.noneEligible: No projects are ready for People's Choice right now. Check back once more projects hit their readiness bars.
pc.votes: {n, plural, =0 {No votes yet} one {# vote} other {# votes}}
pc.votedThisProject: You've backed this project. Thanks for your vote.
pc.votedThanks: Vote counted — thanks for backing this one.
pc.backSub: This project is ready. Add your vote to push it toward the wildcard spotlight.
pc.explainer: Only ready projects (real goal, crew, tasks, a build-log entry) can be backed, so votes can never push an empty project into the spotlight. You get one vote per project, and votes never reset. When the slot is free, the most-backed ready project takes it once it has at least {n} votes — and keeps it until the project finishes. A tie at the top waits for more votes to break it. It's the only slot a steward can cancel.

succession.successorSub: Who takes over if you step away. Required on six-month and one-year pools; optional but recommended on shorter ones — it lets you hand off cleanly without the project going up for adoption.
succession.stepBackWithSuccessor: You named {name} as successor. Stepping back hands them the project — it keeps its place — and gives you a link to send them.
succession.stepBackWithoutSuccessor: No successor named. Stepping back puts the project up for adoption and releases its spotlight slot until someone takes it on.
succession.adoptableSub: The last maintainer stepped back. Adopt it to keep the work alive — you become the maintainer and the project re-enters the running for a spotlight.

errors.auth: That maintainer link is wrong or expired.

form.missing: Please fill in the required fields.
form.video: That doesn't look like a YouTube link. Use a youtube.com or youtu.be URL, or leave it empty.

resume.notfound: No project matches that link or token. Check you copied the whole thing.

crew.already: That email is already on this crew.

task.joinFirst: Join the crew first, then claim with the email you joined with.
task.notApproved: The maintainer hasn't approved you yet. You can claim once approved.
task.notClaimed: That task isn't claimed, so there's nothing to submit.
task.notClaimer: Only the person who claimed this task can report on it. Use the email you claimed with.

adopt.notAdoptable: This project isn't open for adoption.

pc.ineligible: This project isn't eligible for People's Choice right now.
pc.failed: Something went wrong casting your vote. Please try again.
